creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Why MCP and ChatGPT Apps Use Double Iframes — Frédéric Barthelet, Alpic
videos.summary: MCP and ChatGPT apps use double iframe architecture to balance security and functionality. The outer iframe loads a lightweight script from a controlled subdomain, which then loads the actual app HTML via srcdoc into an inner frame, following a pattern Facebook pioneered for their app marketplace. This approach prevents both CSP blocking of third-party scripts and sandbox escapes while isolating per-app storage.

videos.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
videos.summary: Studio is an internal workspace built by WorkOS that lets non-technical users query Snowflake, Linear, and Notion in natural language and generate reusable data widgets. The LLM generates declarative JavaScript widgets that execute deterministically and cheaply on subsequent runs without manual SQL engineering loops.

videos.title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
videos.summary: WebMCP is a proposed web standard from Google Chrome that enables AI agents to interact with websites through structured, named tools rather than DOM parsing and pixel coordinate clicks. It provides both declarative API (HTML attributes) and imperative API (JavaScript registration) for defining agent-callable actions, with early preview support in Chrome 146.

videos.title: Your Attention Is the Bottleneck, Not Your Agents — Zack Proser, WorkOS
videos.summary: Zack Proser presents a framework for sustainable AI-assisted development where human attention is the primary bottleneck rather than agent speed or capability. He proposes a four-layer stack involving signal agents for notification management, verification gates for quality assurance, weekly self-improvement loops over conversation history, and biometric integration to maintain developer wellbeing.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Omnigent: The New Meta-Harness for EVERY Coding Agent - Claude Code, Codex, Pi, More
videos.summary: Omnigent is an open-source meta-harness that orchestrates multiple coding models (Claude Code, Codex, Pi) as interchangeable workers. It routes tasks between models, provides human-in-the-loop governance, and maintains sessions across terminal, browser, and mobile devices.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 model was pulled from availability three days after launch following a US government export-control directive over alleged jailbreak vulnerabilities. The presenter benchmarked Fable 5 before withdrawal and evaluates whether its 2x price premium over Opus 4.8 was justified, discussing implications for developers building on these models.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's Agents CLI is an open-source command-line tool that simplifies building and deploying AI agents to production. It provides agent identity, sandboxed code execution, audit trails, and integrates with AI coding assistants like Claude Code for rapid development and deployment.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin is hosting a live AI Q&A stream on Saturday where viewers can ask questions about AI coding, agents, Claude Code, and Archon. The session doubles as a casual chess-playing hangout with weekly uploads on Wednesday.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: The video explains how to build proactive AI agents using feedback loops to enable self-improvement and autonomous decision-making. It covers practical implementations like autonomous ads optimization and SEO, leveraging memory and tooling frameworks like Loopany to sustain continuous improvement cycles.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses /goal feature and autonomous coder products including Crewlet and Superdesign. The video covers setup, prompting strategies, and long-horizon task execution through goal-buddy and mission exploration features.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: OpenAI Symphony is presented as a new AI coding paradigm that enables automated testing and workflow management. The video demonstrates setup procedures for harness configuration and integration with Symphony for coding tasks.

videos.title: Okay, this unleashed my agent
videos.summary: AI Jason reviews several AI agent architectures and self-improvement mechanisms, highlighting features such as multi-layer memory systems, autoresearch capabilities, and self-evolving skills. The video demonstrates practical agent implementations and comparisons across different frameworks.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3, a series of open models available in Nano, Super, and Ultra variants. The architecture combines hybrid mamba transformer, MTP (Mixture of Tokens and Predictors), and Latent MoE components to distinguish it from other open models.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax M3 is a language model that replaces full attention with MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA). It introduces tiled operations and optimized KV cache reads to reduce latency during prefill and decode stages in inference.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: Pi is described as a unique agent framework that enables various use cases like OpenClaw integration. The video compares Pi's design against other frontier agent tools like Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and Antigravity in terms of handling complex harness layers.

videos.title: Huawei makes 1.4nm chip??
videos.summary: Caleb discusses Huawei's announcement of the Tau Scaling Law as a competitive response in the hardware and semiconductor race between China and the USA. The video covers the role of EUV and DUV lithography, packaging, and logic folding in chip manufacturing advances.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses how AI is reshaping job hunting and hiring in tech for 2026. The video addresses resume screening by AI, the continued value of junior developers despite automation, and recommends networking, personal branding, open-source contributions, and hands-on project building as strategies to stand out.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: AWS Developers presents five production deployment patterns for AI agents: streaming tool call events for UI responsiveness, separated authentication layers using API Gateway and Cognito, type-safe database access with tenant isolation, guardrails and cost controls via lifecycle hooks and model routing, and observability through metrics and OpenTelemetry distributed tracing.

videos.title: Infrastructure as Code for Beginners (AWS CDK)
videos.summary: An AWS Developers educational video introducing Infrastructure as Code fundamentals through a hands-on project. It demonstrates building a serverless REST API using AWS CDK with Python, comparing manual AWS console setup against infrastructure-as-code automation, and covering Lambda, DynamoDB, and API Gateway.

videos.title: Stop AI Hallucinations With These 5 Techniques
videos.summary: AWS Developers presents five techniques to reduce AI hallucinations in agent workflows: semantic tool selection to filter context, graph RAG for precise reasoning, multi-agent validation for response checking, symbolic guardians using Python rules, and runtime guardrails for agent self-correction. The approaches are demonstrated on a travel agent and integrated into Amazon Bedrock AgentCore for production scale.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech examines whether Google engineers use "vibe coding" practices, comparing code generation approaches between OpenAI's Codex and Claude, and exploring vibing trends at Google across 2024-2026. The content covers what counts as vibing and how different companies approach code generation.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech video argues that Malus, a company or tool, is harming or "killing" open source. The video covers what open source is, discusses an AI agent conflict, examines Malus's operations, and debates whether the company is detrimental to open source maintainers and the ecosystem.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Pigment is an AI performance management platform that consolidates finance, supply chain, HR, and sales data into a single system for decision-making. The company has raised over $400 million and serves enterprise customers like Figma, Brex, and Anthropic while growing 2x year over year.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that passionate customers are not proof of product-market fit and that real PMF manifests as customer pull rather than founder push. He advocates embedding in customer workflows over formal interviews and emphasizes betting on future AI capabilities rather than current ones.

videos.title: He Raised $26M Betting on Probabilities, Not Guesses | Koah Labs, Nic Baird
videos.summary: Nic Baird of Koah Labs argues that ad monetization, not subscriptions, is the right model for AI tools and that the real bottleneck for AI agents is human trust rather than technology. He raised $26M for Koah Labs, an ad monetization platform targeting AI tools, betting against the 2024 VC consensus favoring subscriptions.

videos.title: I Hit $1M ARR in 117 Days. Bootstrapped to $10M. Here's My Playbook | Chatbase, Yasser Elsaid
videos.summary: Yasser Elsaid, founder of Chatbase, shares his bootstrapping playbook for achieving $1M ARR in 117 days and scaling to $10M with no funding. The interview covers growth strategies including product-led growth, SEO, warm outbound, pricing experimentation, churn reduction, and decision-making frameworks for capital raises and team structure.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
videos.summary: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

videos.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
